publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Regression; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Regression: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A positive R-squared or lower RMSE proves accurate predictions for every case and explains which features cause the target.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.

answer.explanation: Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.

items.title: Regression: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'predictive association'. Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; [[checkpoint]] does not establish causation.

answer.explanation: Compare a baseline with decision-relevant error metrics, uncertainty, residual patterns, and slices; predictive association does not establish causation.

items.title: Regression: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read n, n true targets, and n predictions. Print model MAE, mean-baseline MAE, and model or baseline for the lower error; ties choose baseline.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    values = list(map(float, data.split())); n = int(values[0])
    truth = values[1:1+n]; predicted = values[1+n:1+2*n]
    model_mae = sum(abs(a-b) for a,b in zip(truth, predicted)) / n
    mean = sum(truth) / n
    baseline_mae = sum(abs(value-mean) for value in truth) / n
    winner = 'model' if model_mae < baseline_mae else 'baseline'
    return f"{model_mae:.3f} {baseline_mae:.3f} {winner}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The model is compared with a simple baseline using a declared scale-sensitive error summary; no causal or slice claim is inferred.
